# UX & Product Report — myAgentic-IT-Project-team — 2026-03-07

> **Mode:** AUDIT | **Generated by:** Synthesis Agent (17) | **Cycle:** COMBO_AUDIT (TECH + UX)
> **Source phase:** Phase 3 — Experience Design
> **Source agents:** 10 (UX Researcher), 11 (UX Designer), 12 (UI Designer), 13 (Accessibility Specialist), 32 (Content Strategist), 35 (Localization Specialist)
> **Critic validation:** APPROVED (2025-01-20) — all 6 agents individually approved, 0 cross-agent contradictions

---

## 1. Summary for this team

The myAgentic-IT-Project-team webapp is a **single-page application** (~2,200 LOC `index.html`) providing a management interface for questionnaires, decisions, and AI-agent pipeline orchestration. The UX audit evaluated the existing interface across usability, interaction design, visual design, accessibility, content quality, and internationalization readiness.

**Current state assessment:** The application provides a **functional but immature** user experience. The card-based layout is clean with reasonable whitespace, and basic keyboard navigation (F1 help, tab management) is implemented. However, the audit identified **53 findings** across 6 UX disciplines, revealing systemic gaps in error recovery, design system maturity, accessibility compliance, content governance, and internationalization.

**#1 Critical Finding (4-agent consensus):** The error recovery pattern `toast('Failed: ' + e.message)` is unanimously identified as the highest-priority UX issue by Agents 10 (friction point), 11 (Nielsen Heuristic #9 CRITICAL), 13 (WCAG 3.3.1/3.3.3 failure), and 32 (poor microcopy pattern). This is the strongest cross-agent consensus in the entire audit cycle.

**Key strengths:** Clean card-based layout, consistent CSS variable theming for colors (95% tokenized), working keyboard shortcuts and tab management, responsive layout with media breakpoints, `<html lang="en">` and UTF-8 charset correctly declared, skip-navigation link present.

**Key weaknesses:** No design system (6 token categories missing), no onboarding flow, no empirical user research or analytics, WCAG 2.1 AA non-conformant (7 failures), no content style guide, i18n readiness score 2.25/10 (all strings hardcoded), no RTL support.

---

## 2. Recommendations (prioritized)

| Priority | Recommendation | Source | Effort | Impact |
|----------|----------------|--------|--------|--------|
| **P1** | Implement error recovery framework — friendly messages, retry mechanism, recovery guidance, persistent server-unreachable banner | Agent 11 (REC-UXD-001), Agent 13 (REC-A11Y-001), Agent 32 (REC-CNT-002), Agent 10 (REC-UXR-004) | M (8 SP) | CRITICAL — 4-agent consensus, WCAG + Nielsen #9 |
| **P1** | Establish complete design token system (spacing, shadows, motion, z-index, type scale) + export design-tokens.json | Agent 12 (REC-UID-001) | S (6 SP) | HIGH — architectural prerequisite for all visual work |
| **P1** | Fix WCAG AA contrast failures — muted text (4.5:1) and non-text borders (3:1) | Agent 13 (REC-A11Y-002), Agent 12 (REC-UID-003) | S (2 SP) | HIGH — legal compliance (EAA/ADA conditional) |
| **P1** | Fix emoji accessibility — add aria-hidden, provide text-only accessible names | Agent 13 (REC-A11Y-003) | S (2 SP) | HIGH — WCAG 1.1.1, 2.5.3, 4.1.2 failures |
| **P1** | Establish content style guide with voice, tone, terminology standards | Agent 32 (REC-CNT-001) | S (3 SP) | HIGH — foundational for all content work |
| **P1** | Implement error prevention UX — inline field validation, beforeunload warning | Agent 11 (REC-UXD-002) | S (2 SP) | HIGH — Nielsen #5 |
| **P1** | Implement analytics and telemetry layer | Agent 10 (REC-UXR-001) | S (3 SP) | HIGH — all KPI baselines are INSUFFICIENT_DATA without this |
| **P1** | Implement i18n string externalization architecture | Agent 35 (REC-L10N-001) | M (8 SP) | HIGH — prerequisite for any localization; all 130+ strings hardcoded |
| **P2** | Implement system status visibility — loading indicator, last-refresh timestamp, connection dot | Agent 11 (REC-UXD-003) | S (2 SP) | MEDIUM — Nielsen #1 |
| **P2** | Build first-run onboarding wizard + help tour | Agent 10 (REC-UXR-002), Agent 32 (REC-CNT-003) | M (6 SP) | MEDIUM — no onboarding exists |
| **P2** | Replace emoji icons with SVG icon system | Agent 12 (REC-UID-002) | S (3 SP) | MEDIUM — consistent rendering, screenreader-friendly |
| **P2** | Reduce questionnaire cognitive load — jump-to-unanswered, collapsible sections, progress count | Agent 11 (REC-UXD-004) | S (4 SP) | MEDIUM — cognitive load 6/10 |
| **P2** | Implement locale-aware date/number formatting (Intl API) | Agent 35 (REC-L10N-002) | S (2 SP) | MEDIUM — date format ambiguity risk |
| **P2** | Implement loading skeletons + empty state components | Agent 12 (REC-UID-004) | S (2 SP) | MEDIUM |
| **P2** | Reduce command launch friction from 6→3 steps | Agent 10 (REC-UXR-005) | S (3 SP) | MEDIUM |
| **P2** | Add motion/contrast preference support (prefers-reduced-motion, forced-colors) | Agent 13 (REC-A11Y-004) | S (2 SP) | MEDIUM — accessibility enhancement |
| **P2** | Standardize success/confirmation copy | Agent 32 (REC-CNT-004) | S (1 SP) | LOW — voice consistency |
| **P2** | Browser history state management (SPA hash routing) | Agent 11 (REC-UXD-005) | S (2 SP) | LOW — Nielsen #3 |
| **P2** | Migrate CSS to logical properties (RTL readiness) | Agent 35 (REC-L10N-003) | M (3 SP) | LOW — future-proofing for RTL markets |
| **P2** | Establish translation management workflow + glossary | Agent 35 (REC-L10N-004) | S (3 SP) | LOW — process foundation for localization |
| **P2** | Questionnaire sidebar search | Agent 10 (REC-UXR-003) | S (1 SP) | LOW |
| **P3** | Medium-priority accessibility enhancements — help links, page title update, font size, aria-live refactor, colorblind indicators | Agent 13 (REC-A11Y-005) | M (4.5 SP) | MEDIUM — progressive improvement |

## 3. Roadmap items for this team (12 months)

| Quarter | Item | Dependent on | KPI target |
|---------|------|--------------|------------|
| **Q1** | Sprint 1: Foundation & critical fixes — design token system, content style guide, analytics setup, error recovery framework, WCAG contrast fixes, emoji accessibility, error prevention UX, inline validation | NONE (all prerequisite work) | Design tokens: 6/6 categories; WCAG contrast: 0 failures; Error recovery: all messages user-friendly |
| **Q1** | Sprint 2: Interaction & status — system status visibility, onboarding wizard, questionnaire cognitive load reduction, loading skeletons, SVG icon system, command flow simplification | Sprint 1 (design tokens + style guide prerequisite) | Onboarding: exists; Cognitive load: ≤5/10 per view |
| **Q2** | Sprint 3: i18n architecture — string externalization, locale resource files (HTML + JS), Intl.DateTimeFormat/NumberFormat, CSS logical properties migration | Sprint 1 (design tokens) | i18n readiness: ≥7/10; Hardcoded strings: 0 |
| **Q2** | Sprint 4: Content & refinement — onboarding content briefs, success copy standardization, browser history management, TMS workflow, domain glossary | Sprint 2 (onboarding UX) + Sprint 3 (i18n architecture) | Content governance: style guide active; TMS: configured |
| **Q3** | Sprint 5: Accessibility enhancements — prefers-reduced-motion, forced-colors, help panel keyboard, page title, font size, aria-live refactor, colorblind indicators | Sprint 1 (WCAG critical fixes) | WCAG AA: full conformance (0 failures) |
| **Q3** | Sprint 6: Usability validation — first usability testing round, SUS survey, analytics review, persona validation | Sprint 1 (analytics) + Sprint 2 (onboarding) | SUS score: baseline established; Task success rate: measured |
| **Q4** | Sprint 7: Localization pilot — first language translation (NL or DE), post-edit review, cultural adaptation | Sprint 3 (i18n architecture) + Q-L10N-001 answered | First locale: deployed; Translation quality: validated |
| **Q4** | Sprint 8: Iteration — address usability test findings, second round of UX improvements, accessibility regression testing | Sprint 6 (usability data) | SUS score: ≥68; WCAG AA: maintained |

| Cognitive load (questionnaire view) | 6/10 HEURISTIC (source: Agent 11) | ≤5/10 | ≤4/10 | Heuristic + empirical evaluation |
| i18n readiness score | 2.25/10 (source: Agent 35) | ≥7/10 | 9/10 | 8-criteria audit |

| Error message quality | 0% user-friendly (source: Agent 32) | 100% user-friendly | 100% | Content audit |

## 5. ⚠️ Blockers from other teams (ACTION REQUIRED)

> This team **cannot start** the items below without input or a decision from another team.

| Blocker ID | Blocking team | What is needed | Priority | Recommended deadline |
|------------|--------------|----------------|----------|---------------------|
| BLK-U-001 | **Tech** | Atomic file writes (REC-SEC-002, REC-DATA-005) must be implemented before UX team can address concurrent editing scenarios (Agent 11 QUE-UXD-MULTIUSER-001). Without atomic writes, auto-save and multi-user features risk data corruption. | HIGH | Before UX Sprint 2 |
| BLK-U-002 | **Tech** | Server-side error response structure must be standardized (REC-DEV-008 error wrapping) before UX can implement the full error recovery framework. UX can define the framework and client-side patterns, but the actual error codes/messages depend on Tech implementation. | MEDIUM | Before UX Sprint 2 |
| BLK-U-003 | **Tech** | i18n string externalization requires a server-side locale negotiation endpoint or at minimum a build-time bundling approach. Tech must agree on the i18n architecture pattern (REC-L10N-001 is scoped as OUT_OF_SCOPE: TECH by Agent 35). | HIGH | Before UX Sprint 3 |
| BLK-U-004 | **Tech** | Analytics/telemetry endpoint (REC-UXR-001, SP-UXR-1-002) requires a server-side API to persist event data. Tech must implement or approve the endpoint design. | MEDIUM | Before UX Sprint 1 (start; can prototype client-side first) |

**Note:** UX Sprint 1 design token work (REC-UID-001), content style guide (REC-CNT-001), WCAG contrast fixes (REC-A11Y-002), and emoji accessibility (REC-A11Y-003) have **no Tech blockers** and can proceed immediately.

## 6. Alignment desired with other teams (ADVISORY)

| Item | Involved team | Reason | Urgency |
|------|--------------|--------|---------|
| Error message framework → error handler implementation | Tech (Agent 06 REC-DEV-008, Agent 08 REC-SEC-001) | UX defines the messages and patterns; Tech implements the handler code | HIGH — should align in Sprint 1 |
| Design tokens → CSS variable naming conventions | Tech (if build tooling is involved) | Token naming must be consistent across frontend and any build pipeline | MEDIUM — align during Sprint 1 |
| i18n architecture → server configuration | Tech (Agent 05 architecture decisions) | String externalization approach affects both frontend and backend | HIGH — must agree before Sprint 3 |
| Accessibility testing → CI pipeline integration | Tech (Agent 07 CI pipeline) | Automated accessibility tests (axe-core) should run in CI | LOW — align during Sprint 2 |
| Onboarding wizard → data model | Tech (session state management) | Onboarding completion state must persist (server-side or localStorage) | LOW — align during Sprint 2 |
| Content governance → deployment process | Tech (CI/CD) | If content is externalized, content updates may need their own deployment path | LOW — future consideration |

## 8. Guardrails for this team

### UX Research Guardrails (Agent 10)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| G-UXR-001 | All UX claims labeled by evidence source (HEURISTIC/ANALYTICS/USABILITY_TEST/INTERVIEW) | Require label before merge |
| G-UXR-002 | Primary flow ≤3 steps max | Block PR if new flow exceeds |
| G-UXR-003 | Telemetry must not collect PII | Block PR; escalate to security |

### UX Design Guardrails (Agent 11)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| G-UXD-001 | All error states must provide recovery path | Block PR |
| G-UXD-002 | Cognitive load ≤6/10 per view | Block PR; require simplification |
| G-UXD-003 | Inline validation on all form inputs | Block PR |

### UI Design Guardrails (Agent 12)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| G-UID-001 | Spacing, shadow, motion, z-index must use design tokens | Block PR |
| G-UID-002 | Text colors must meet WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 minimum) | Block PR |

### Accessibility Guardrails (Agent 13)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| G-A11Y-001 | Accessible name must match/contain visible label text | Block PR |
| G-A11Y-002 | Text contrast ≥4.5:1 on every background | Block PR |
| G-A11Y-003 | Status changes must have aria-live announcement | Block PR |
| G-A11Y-004 | CSS must respect prefers-reduced-motion + forced-colors | Block PR |

### Content Guardrails (Agent 32)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| G-CNT-AUDIT-001 | Error messages follow established framework (no raw API errors) | Block PR |
| G-CNT-AUDIT-002 | All new UI copy conforms to Content Style Guide | Block PR |
| G-CNT-AUDIT-003 | Confirmation buttons use action-specific labels | Block PR |

### Localization Guardrails (Agent 35)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| G-L10N-001 | No hardcoded UI strings (must use i18n key lookup) | Block PR |
| G-L10N-002 | Locale-aware formatting for all date/number values (Intl API) | Block PR |
| G-L10N-003 | CSS logical properties for new layout code | Block PR |
